意图好奇.name: 意图好奇
意图好奇.concept: Ask about the child's creative intent or purpose behind the object — why they chose it, made it, or what they imagine it doing.
examples: Did you make this yourself? What were you trying to build?
What do you want this to do when you grow up?
Is this from a story you made up?
意图好奇.note: Best when the object is child-created; always apply ×0.5 weight penalty in selector

想象导向.name: 想象导向
想象导向.concept: Pull the real object into a fantasy or magical world — invite the child to imagine a scenario where the object transforms or goes on an adventure.
examples: If this toy dog could fly, where would it go first?
What if these flowers were magic — what power would they have?
Imagine this T-Rex is alive — what would it eat for breakfast?

情绪投射.name: 情绪投射
情绪投射.concept: Project emotions onto the object — treat it as if it has feelings and ask the child how the object feels right now.
examples: Do you think your toy dog is happy today?
If this flower could talk, would it say it feels lonely or loved?
Does your dinosaur look scared or excited to you?

角色代入.name: 角色代入
角色代入.concept: Put the child into an interactive role with the object — make them a character in a mini-story alongside it.
examples: If you were a tiny person living inside this flower, what would your house look like?
Pretend you're the T-Rex's best friend — where would you go together?
If this toy dog was YOUR pet for a day, what would you feed it?

选择偏好.name: 选择偏好
选择偏好.concept: Invite the child to express a personal like, dislike, or preference about the object — no right or wrong answer.
examples: Is this your favorite toy, or do you have an even more special one?
Do you love dinosaurs, or do you like other animals more?
Which color flower do you like best — this one or a red one?

细节发现.name: 细节发现
细节发现.concept: Point out ONE specific sensory or visual detail of the object and invite the child to notice or react to it — no teaching, just shared discovery.
examples: Look at how tiny its paws are — have you ever noticed that before?
The petals feel so soft, don't they? Did you touch it?
Those spiky plates on its back look so cool — what do you think they're for?

经验、生活链接.name: 经验、生活链接
经验、生活链接.concept: Connect the object to something from the child's own life or experiences mentioned earlier in the conversation.
examples: You mentioned you have a dog at home — is this toy anything like your real dog?
Last time you talked about loving the park — did you find this there?
This reminds me of what you said before — do you collect these?

创意改造.name: 创意改造
创意改造.concept: Encourage the child to imagine redesigning or upgrading the object — what would they change if they could?
examples: If you could add one superpower to this toy dog, what would it be?
If you were the designer, would you make this flower a different color?
What would you add to this T-Rex to make it even more amazing?
